steps.title: Convert to vertex form by completing the square
steps.content:
Factor the leading coefficient from the first two terms:

$$p(x) = 2(x^2 + 10x) + 15$$

Complete the square inside the parentheses by adding and subtracting $(\frac{10}{2})^2 = 25$:

$$= 2(x^2 + 10x + 25 - 25) + 15$$

$$= 2((x + 5)^2 - 25) + 15$$

$$= 2(x + 5)^2 - 50 + 15$$

$$= 2(x + 5)^2 - 35$$

steps.title: Identify the minimum from vertex form
steps.content: The vertex form is $p(x) = 2(x + 5)^2 - 35$, which shows the vertex at $(-5, -35)$. Since the coefficient of the squared term is positive, the parabola opens upward and has a minimum value of $-35$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
